2420	\begin{lemma}[Right adjoint of a mono-preserving left adjoint preserves injectives]
2421	  \label{lem:injective_of_adjoint}
2422	  \lean{CategoryTheory.Injective.injective_of_adjoint}
2423	  \mathlibok
2424	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
2425	  (\texttt{Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Preadditive.Injective.Basic}).}
2426	  Let \(L \dashv R\) be an adjunction between abelian (or, more generally, suitable)
2427	  categories such that the left adjoint \(L\) preserves monomorphisms. Then for every
2428	  injective object \(J\) of the target, \(R(J)\) is an injective object of the source.
2429	\end{lemma}

2431	\begin{lemma}[Sheafification is left adjoint to the inclusion of sheaves of modules]
2432	  \label{lem:mod_pmod_adjunction}
2433	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction}
2434	  \mathlibok
2435	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib
2436	  (\texttt{Mathlib.Algebra.Category.ModuleCat.Presheaf.Sheafification}).}
2437	  For the identity ring map \(\alpha = \mathbf{1}\) over a scheme \(X\),
2438	  sheafification is left adjoint to the inclusion
2439	  \(\mathrm{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X) \hookrightarrow \mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\) of
2440	  sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules into presheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules:
2441	  \[
2442	    \operatorname{sheafification} \;\dashv\; \iota,
2443	    \qquad
2444	    \iota = \text{the inclusion } X.\mathrm{Modules} \hookrightarrow
2445	    X.\mathrm{PresheafOfModules}.
2446	  \]
2447	  The left adjoint sheafification is exact, in particular it preserves
2448	  monomorphisms.
2449	\end{lemma}

2517	\begin{lemma}
2518	[Hom into an injective preserves exactness and quasi-isomorphisms]
2519	  \label{lem:hom_into_injective_exact}
2520	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.preadditiveYoneda_obj_preservesFiniteColimits_of_injective,
2521	    AlgebraicGeometry.quasiIso_map_preadditiveYoneda_of_injective}

2523	  Let \(\mathcal{A}\) be an abelian category and \(I\) an injective object. Then the
2524	  contravariant hom functor \(\operatorname{Hom}(-, I) =
2525	  \operatorname{preadditiveYoneda}.\mathrm{obj}\,I : \mathcal{A}^{\mathrm{op}} \to
2526	  \mathrm{Ab}\) is exact: it is automatically left exact (preserves finite limits), and
2527	  injectivity of \(I\) makes it carry epimorphisms to epimorphisms, so it preserves
2528	  finite colimits (\(\operatorname{preadditiveYoneda\_obj\_preservesFiniteColimits\_of\_injective}\))
2529	  and hence preserves homology. Consequently \(\operatorname{Hom}(-, I)\) carries
2530	  quasi-isomorphisms of chain complexes in \(\mathcal{A}^{\mathrm{op}}\) to
2531	  quasi-isomorphisms of the \(\operatorname{Hom}\)-cochain complexes
2532	  (\(\operatorname{quasiIso\_map\_preadditiveYoneda\_of\_injective}\)). Both statements
2533	  are for a general abelian category, the right generality for reuse in
2534	  Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_cech_acyclic}. The mechanism --- a right adjoint / exact
2535	  contravariant functor preserves the relevant exactness --- is the same homological
2536	  principle behind Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_of_adjoint}.
2537	\end{lemma}

2539	\begin{proof}
2540	  \uses{lem:injective_of_adjoint}

2542	  \(\operatorname{Injective.injective\_iff\_preservesEpimorphisms\_preadditiveYoneda\_obj}\)
2543	  gives that \(\operatorname{preadditiveYoneda}.\mathrm{obj}\,I\) preserves
2544	  epimorphisms. Combined with the automatic preservation of finite limits, the
2545	  short-exact-sequence criterion
2546	  \(\operatorname{Functor.preservesFiniteColimits\_iff\_forall\_exact\_map\_and\_epi}\)
2547	  shows it preserves finite colimits, hence (being also left exact) it has
2548	  \(\operatorname{PreservesHomology}\) by
2549	  \(\operatorname{Functor.preservesHomologyOfExact}\). A homology-preserving functor
2550	  sends quasi-isomorphisms to quasi-isomorphisms degreewise
2551	  (\(\operatorname{quasiIsoAt\_map\_of\_preservesHomology}\)), which is the second
2552	  statement.

2577	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology,
2578	  \texttt{lemma-injective-trivial-cech}.}
2579	  Let \(X\) be a ringed space, let \(\mathcal{U} : U =
2580	  \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be any open covering, and let \(\mathcal{I}\) be an
2581	  \emph{injective} \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. Then the {\v C}ech cohomology of
2582	  \(\mathcal{I}\) is concentrated in degree zero:
2583	  \[
2584	    \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) =
2585	    \begin{cases}
2586	      \mathcal{I}(U) & p = 0, \\
2587	      0 & p > 0.
2588	    \end{cases}
2589	  \]
2590	\end{lemma}

2592	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "An injective $\mathcal{O}_X$-module is also injective as an
2593	% object in
2594	% the category $\textit{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)$ (for example since
2595	% sheafification is an exact left adjoint to the inclusion functor,
2596	% using Homology, Lemma \ref{homology-lemma-adjoint-preserve-injectives}).
2597	% Hence we can apply Lemma \ref{lemma-cech-cohomology-derived-presheaves}
2598	% (or its proof) to see the result."
2599	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "Let $\mathcal{I}$ be an injective presheaf of
2600	% $\mathcal{O}_X$-modules.
2601	% In this case the functor $\Hom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(-, \mathcal{I})$
2602	% is exact on $\textit{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)$. By
2603	% Lemma \ref{lemma-cech-map-into} we have
2604	% $$
2605	% \Hom_{\mathcal{O}_X}(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet, \mathcal{I})
2606	% =
2607	% \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}).
2608	% $$
2609	% By Lemma \ref{lemma-homology-complex} we have that $K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet$ is
2610	% quasi-isomorphic to $\mathcal{O}_\mathcal{U}[0]$. Hence by
2611	% the exactness of Hom into $\mathcal{I}$ mentioned above we see
2612	% that $\check{H}^i(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = 0$ for all
2613	% $i > 0$."
2614	\begin{proof}
2615	  \uses{def:cech_complex, def:section_cech_complex, lem:cech_complex_hom_identification, lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso, lem:injective_of_adjoint, lem:mod_pmod_adjunction, lem:cech_complex_op_identification, lem:section_cech_complex_mapop_iso, lem:hom_into_injective_exact}
2616	  The argument has two independent parts.
2617	
2618	  \emph{Part 1: an injective sheaf is injective as a presheaf.} Sheafification is
2619	  left adjoint to the inclusion
2620	  \(\iota : \mathrm{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X) \hookrightarrow \mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\)
2621	  of sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules into presheaves of
2622	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules (Lemma~\ref{lem:mod_pmod_adjunction}), and its left
2623	  adjoint sheafification is exact, hence preserves monomorphisms. By
2624	  Lemma~\ref{lem:injective_of_adjoint} the right adjoint \(\iota\) carries injective
2625	  objects to injective objects; thus an injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module
2626	  \(\mathcal{I}\) is injective as an object of \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\). In
2627	  particular the contravariant functor \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(-,
2628	  \mathcal{I})\) is exact on \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\).
2629	
2630	  \emph{Part 2: vanishing.} By Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso} the free
2631	  presheaf complex \(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet\) of
2632	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_free_presheaf_complex} is a resolution of
2633	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}\) concentrated in degree \(0\); equivalently the
2634	  augmented complex \(\cdots \to K(\mathcal{U})_1 \to K(\mathcal{U})_0 \to
2635	  \mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}} \to 0\) is exact in \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\).
2636	  Applying the exact functor \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(-, \mathcal{I})\)
2637	  of Part 1 to this exact augmented complex yields an exact complex; by the
2638	  identification \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(K(\mathcal{U})_\bullet,
2639	  \mathcal{I}) = \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I})\) of
2640	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_complex_hom_identification} (with the section {\v C}ech complex
2641	  of Definition~\ref{def:section_cech_complex}), this is precisely the statement that
2642	  the section {\v C}ech complex of \(\mathcal{I}\) is exact in positive degrees, with
2643	  \(\operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_X}(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}, \mathcal{I}) =
2644	  \mathcal{I}(U)\) supplying degree \(0\).
2645	
2646	  \emph{Categorical assembly.} Concretely the exactness is produced by the
2647	  opposite-transport bridge. The augmentation \(\operatorname{cechFreeComplexAug}
2648	  \mathcal{U}\) is a quasi-isomorphism (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso});
2649	  taking its opposite via the \(\operatorname{HomologicalComplex.op}\) machinery and
2650	  applying \(\operatorname{preadditiveYoneda}.\mathrm{obj}\,\mathcal{I}\) keeps it a
2651	  quasi-isomorphism, since \(\mathcal{I}\) is injective as a presheaf of modules
2652	  (Part 1) and \(\operatorname{Hom}(-, \mathcal{I})\) then preserves homology
2653	  (\(\operatorname{quasiIso\_map\_preadditiveYoneda\_of\_injective}\),
2654	  Lemma~\ref{lem:hom_into_injective_exact}). Transporting source and target of this
2655	  mapped-opposite quasi-isomorphism across the opposite-transport isos
2656	  \(\operatorname{homCechComplexMapOpIso}\)
2657	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_complex_op_identification}) and
2658	  \(\operatorname{sectionCechComplexMapOpIso}\)
2659	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:section_cech_complex_mapop_iso}) identifies it with the augmentation
2660	  of the section {\v C}ech complex \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U},
2661	  \mathcal{I})\) onto the mapped image of the degree-\(0\)-concentrated target
2662	  \(\mathcal{O}_{\mathcal{U}}[0]\). The latter has vanishing homology in positive
2663	  degrees, so the section {\v C}ech complex does too. Hence
2664	  \(\check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) for all \(p > 0\) and
2665	  \(\check{H}^0(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{I}) = \mathcal{I}(U)\). This direct route uses
2666	  neither enough-injectives in \(\mathrm{PMod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\) nor a right-derived
2667	  \(\delta\)-functor identification.
2668	\end{proof}

2973	The comparison is proved by the \emph{acyclic-resolution} (Cartan--Leray) route. The
2974	augmented {\v C}ech complex is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) whose terms are

2976	Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived} then identifies the derived
2977	functor with the cohomology of the complex obtained by applying \(f_*\). The two
2978	geometric inputs are isolated in the following sub-lemmas.

3021	\begin{proof}

3023	  Exactness of a complex of sheaves is a local question on \(X\), so it may be
3024	  checked on the stalks, equivalently over each affine open of a basis. Restricting
3025	  to an affine open \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\) on which \(\mathcal{F}|_U =
3026	  \widetilde{M}\) and over which the cover restricts to a standard cover \(U =
3027	  \bigcup D(f_i)\), the augmented {\v C}ech complex becomes the extended complex of
3028	  localisations

3030	  \cdots\). Its exactness is exactly the standard-cover vanishing of
3031	  Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_acyclic_affine}, witnessed by the prime-local contracting
3032	  homotopy \(h(s)_{i_0 \ldots i_p} = s_{i_{\mathrm{fix}} i_0 \ldots i_p}\). As this
3033	  holds over every affine of the basis, the augmented complex is exact on \(X\).

3038	  \label{def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology}
3039	  \lean{PresheafOfModules.homologyIsoSheafify,
3040	    PresheafOfModules.counitComplexIso,
3041	    PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdditive,
3042	    CategoryTheory.Functor.mapHomologyIso'}
3043	  % NOTE: project-built generalization. Stacks Tag~01XJ
3044	  % (\texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}) establishes the consequence
3045	  % $R^if_*\mathcal{F} \cong \text{sheafification of }(V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V),\mathcal{F}))$
3046	  % but does not state sheafification commuting with the homology of an arbitrary
3047	  % cochain complex as a standalone lemma. No verbatim Stacks source quote for this block.
3048	  Let \(K\) be a cochain complex of presheaves of modules over a site admitting
3049	  sheafification. For every index \(i\) there is a natural
3050	  isomorphism between the \(i\)-th homology of the sheafified complex and the
3051	  sheafification of the \(i\)-th objectwise (presheaf-level) homology of \(K\),
3052	  \[
3053	    H^i\bigl(\operatorname{sheafify}(K)\bigr)
3054	      \;\cong\;
3055	    \operatorname{sheafify}\bigl(H^i(K)\bigr).
3056	  \]
3057	  Equivalently, sheafification commutes with the formation of homology. This is the
3058	  reusable engine underlying the presheaf description of the higher direct images
3059	  (Stacks Tag~01XJ): it is what turns the resolution-internal cohomology sheaf into a
3060	  sheafification of an explicit cohomology presheaf.
3061	\end{lemma}
3062	
3063	\begin{proof}
3064	  Sheafification is exact: as the left adjoint of the inclusion of sheaves into
3065	  presheaves it preserves all colimits, and on a site admitting sheafification it
3066	  additionally preserves finite limits, so it preserves both the kernels and the
3067	  cokernels through which homology is computed. An exact additive functor commutes with
3068	  the homology functor of a cochain complex; chasing this through the counit complex
3069	  isomorphism \(\operatorname{sheafify}(\iota K) \cong K\) (the inclusion–sheafification
3070	  adjunction counit, an isomorphism on sheaves) gives
3071	  \(H^i(K) \cong H^i(\operatorname{sheafify} K) \cong
3072	  \operatorname{sheafify}(H^i(K))\). The supporting bricks are the
3073	  additivity of sheafification, the counit complex isomorphism, and the
3074	  complex-level transport of the short-complex map–homology isomorphism along an
3075	  additive homology-preserving functor.
3076	\end{proof}

3078	\begin{lemma}
3079	[Presheaf description of the higher direct images]
3080	  \label{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}
3081	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage_iso_sheafify_presheafHomology,
3082	    AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardResolutionPresheafComplex}
3083	  \uses{def:higher_direct_image, def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology}
3084	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology, Tag 01XJ, Lemma
3085	  % \texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images} (the higher direct images are the
3086	  % sheafifications of the cohomology presheaves of preimages) (read from
3087	  % references/stacks-cohomology.tex, L591--603).
3088	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to Y$ be a morphism of ringed spaces.
3089	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
3090	  % The sheaves $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ are the sheaves
3091	  % associated to the presheaves
3092	  % $$
3093	  % V \longmapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F})
3094	  % $$
3095	  % with restriction mappings as in Equation (\ref{equation-restriction-mapping}).
3096	  % There is a similar statement for $R^if_*$ applied to a
3097	  % bounded below complex $\mathcal{F}^\bullet$."
3098	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology, Tag 01XJ
3099	  (\texttt{lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}).}
3100	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a morphism of schemes and \(\mathcal{G}\) an
3101	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and assume the category of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules has
3102	  enough injective objects, so that the derived pushforward \(R^k f_*\) is defined.
3103	  Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
3104	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules and push it forward degreewise to obtain the cochain complex
3105	  of presheaves of modules \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) on \(S\). For every \(k \geq 0\)
3106	  the higher direct image \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G}\) is isomorphic to the
3107	  \emph{sheafification of the presheaf of objectwise homology} of this pushed complex,
3108	  \[
3109	    R^k f_*\mathcal{G}
3110	      \;\cong\;
3111	    \operatorname{sheafify}\Bigl(\,V \longmapsto
3112	      H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr)\,\Bigr),
3113	    \qquad V \subseteq S \text{ open},
3114	  \]
3115	  the sheafification of the \(k\)-th presheaf-level homology of
3116	  \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\). As a closing remark, this gives the affine-local
3117	  vanishing criterion consumed downstream
3118	  (Lemmas~\ref{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp} and
3119	  \ref{lem:cech_term_pushforward_acyclic}): since the sheafification of a presheaf
3120	  vanishes exactly when that presheaf vanishes on a basis of opens,
3121	  \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G} = 0\) iff \(H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr) = 0\)
3122	  for every \(V\) in a basis of the topology of \(S\); the affine opens form such a
3123	  basis, so it suffices to check the vanishing on affine \(V\).
3124	
3125	  The identification of the displayed presheaf homology with the absolute cohomology
3126	  of the preimage,
3127	  \[
3128	    H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr)
3129	      \;=\;
3130	    H^k\bigl(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}(V)}\bigr),
3131	  \]
3132	  which uses that \(\mathcal{I}^\bullet|_{f^{-1}(V)}\) is again injective over
3133	  \(f^{-1}(V)\), is supplied at point of use; this lemma records the
3134	  resolution-internal sheafify-of-objectwise-homology form.
3135	\end{lemma}
3136	
3137	\begin{proof}
3138	  \uses{def:higher_direct_image, def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology}
3139	  Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{G} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
3140	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules. By definition \(R^k f_*\mathcal{G}\) is the \(k\)-th
3141	  cohomology sheaf of the complex \(f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) of presheaves of modules
3142	  over \(S\). The comparison engine of
3143	  Lemma~\ref{def:cohomology_sheaf_is_sheafify_homology} now applies: taking homology in
3144	  \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules is the sheafification of the objectwise (presheaf-level)
3145	  homology, because the sheafification/inclusion adjunction is exact (it preserves the
3146	  finite limits and colimits computing kernels and cokernels). Hence
3147	  \[
3148	    R^k f_*\mathcal{G}
3149	      \;\cong\;
3150	    \operatorname{sheafify}\Bigl(\,V \longmapsto
3151	      H^k\bigl((f_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)(V)\bigr)\,\Bigr),
3152	  \]
3153	  which is the stated isomorphism. Since the sheafification of a presheaf vanishes
3154	  exactly when the underlying presheaf vanishes on a basis of opens, this yields the
3155	  affine-local vanishing criterion.
3156	\end{proof}

3158	\begin{lemma}
3159	[Affine open immersions are $(\cdot)_*$-acyclic and degenerate the composition]
3160	  \label{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp}
3161	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.higherDirectImage_openImmersion_comp}
3162	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}
3163	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
3164	  % \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing} (relative vanishing of higher direct
3165	  % images for affine morphisms) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
3166	  % L180--199).
3167	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.
3168	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
3169	  % If $f$ is affine then $R^if_*\mathcal{F} = 0$ for all $i > 0$."
3170	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
3171	  \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing}.}
3172	  Let \(X\) be separated, \(j : U \hookrightarrow X\) the open immersion of an affine
3173	  open \(U\), and \(f : X \to S\) any morphism, with composite \(g := f \circ j : U
3174	  \to S\). Then:
3175	  \begin{enumerate}
3176	    \item \(j\) is an affine morphism (an open immersion of an affine open into a
3177	      separated scheme), so its higher direct images vanish: \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H} = 0\)
3178	      for all \(q \geq 1\) and every quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-module
3179	      \(\mathcal{H}\); and
3180	    \item consequently, for every quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{H}\) on \(U\) and every
3181	      \(k \geq 0\),
3182	      \[
3183	        R^k f_*\bigl(j_*\mathcal{H}\bigr) \;\cong\; R^k g_*\mathcal{H}.
3184	      \]
3185	  \end{enumerate}
3186	\end{lemma}
3187	
3188	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "According to
3189	% Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}
3190	% the sheaf
3191	% $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
3192	% $V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)})$.
3193	% By assumption, whenever $V$ is affine we have that $f^{-1}(V)$ is
3194	% affine, see Morphisms, Definition \ref{morphisms-definition-affine}.
3195	% By Lemma \ref{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero} we conclude that
3196	% $H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)}) = 0$
3197	% whenever $V$ is affine. Since $S$ has a basis consisting of affine
3198	% opens we win." (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L187--198)
3199	\begin{proof}
3200	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf, lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived}
3201	  (1) An open immersion of an affine open \(U\) into a separated scheme \(X\) is an
3202	  affine morphism: separatedness makes the immersion \(j\) quasi-separated and the
3203	  preimage of any affine open of \(X\) meets \(U\) in an affine, so \(j\) is affine.
3204	  The relative affine vanishing for affine morphisms then gives \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H} =
3205	  0\) for \(q \geq 1\); concretely, by the presheaf description
3206	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}) \(R^q j_*\mathcal{H}\) is the
3207	  sheafification of \(V \mapsto H^q(j^{-1}(V), \mathcal{H})\), and for affine \(V\) the
3208	  preimage \(j^{-1}(V)\) is affine, on which the Serre vanishing of
3209	  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing} kills \(H^q\) for \(q \geq 1\).
3210	
3211	  (2) Choose an injective resolution \(\mathcal{H} \to \mathcal{I}^\bullet\) in
3212	  \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-modules and apply \(j_*\) degreewise. Since each \(\mathcal{I}^n\)
3213	  is \(j_*\)-acyclic by part (1) (\(R^q j_*\mathcal{I}^n = 0\) for \(q \geq 1\)), the
3214	  complex \(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) is a resolution of \(j_*\mathcal{H}\).
3215	
3216	  Each \(j_*\mathcal{I}^n\) is \(f_*\)-acyclic. By the presheaf description
3217	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf}), \(R^k f_*(j_*\mathcal{I}^n)\) is the
3218	  sheafification of
3219	  \[
3220	    V \;\longmapsto\; H^k\bigl(f^{-1}(V), (j_*\mathcal{I}^n)|_{f^{-1}(V)}\bigr)
3221	      \;=\; H^k\bigl(U \cap f^{-1}(V),\, \mathcal{I}^n|_{U \cap f^{-1}(V)}\bigr),
3222	  \]
3223	  the equality because \((j_*\mathcal{I}^n)|_{f^{-1}(V)}\) is the pushforward of
3224	  \(\mathcal{I}^n|_{U \cap f^{-1}(V)}\) along the open immersion
3225	  \(U \cap f^{-1}(V) \hookrightarrow f^{-1}(V)\), which leaves cohomology unchanged. For
3226	  \(V = \operatorname{Spec}(A) \subseteq S\) affine, \(f^{-1}(V)\) is quasi-compact and,
3227	  \(X\) being separated and \(j\) affine, the affine open \(U\) meets it in the affine
3228	  open \(U \cap f^{-1}(V)\); hence by Serre vanishing
3229	  Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing} the cohomology
3230	  \(H^k(U \cap f^{-1}(V),\, \mathcal{I}^n|_{U \cap f^{-1}(V)}) = 0\) for \(k \geq 1\).
3231	  Since the affine opens form a basis of \(S\), this forces
3232	  \(R^k f_*(j_*\mathcal{I}^n) = 0\) for all \(k \geq 1\).
3233	
3234	  Thus \(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) is an \(f_*\)-acyclic resolution of
3235	  \(j_*\mathcal{H}\), and the acyclic-resolution comparison
3236	  Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived} applied with \(G = f_*\) gives,
3237	  for every \(k\),
3238	  \[
3239	    R^k f_*\bigl(j_*\mathcal{H}\bigr)
3240	      \;\cong\; H^k\bigl(f_*(j_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet)\bigr)
3241	      \;=\; H^k\bigl((f \circ j)_*\mathcal{I}^\bullet\bigr)
3242	      \;=\; R^k g_*\mathcal{H},
3243	  \]
3244	  the middle equality because \(f_* \circ j_* = (f \circ j)_* = g_*\), and the last
3245	  because \(\mathcal{I}^\bullet\) is an injective resolution of \(\mathcal{H}\)
3246	  computing \(R^k g_*\mathcal{H}\).
3247	\end{proof}

3249	\begin{lemma}
3250	[Each {\v C}ech term is $f_*$-acyclic]
3251	  \label{lem:cech_term_pushforward_acyclic}
3252	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cechTerm_pushforward_acyclic}
3253	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, def:cech_nerve, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf, lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp}
3254	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
3255	  % \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing} (relative vanishing of higher direct
3256	  % images for affine morphisms) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
3257	  % L180--199).
3258	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.
3259	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
3260	  % If $f$ is affine then $R^if_*\mathcal{F} = 0$ for all $i > 0$."
3261	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes,
3262	  \texttt{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing} (relative affine vanishing).}
3263	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be separated and quasi-compact, \(\mathcal{F}\) a
3264	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and \(\mathfrak{U}\) a finite affine open
3265	  cover of \(X\) with all intersections affine. Then for every \(p \geq 0\) the
3266	  degree-\(p\) {\v C}ech term \(\mathcal{C}^p =
3267	  \prod_{i_0 \ldots i_p} (j_{i_0 \ldots i_p})_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}})\)
3268	  is right \((\,\cdot\,)_*\)-acyclic for the pushforward \(f_*\):
3269	  \[
3270	    (f_*).\mathrm{rightDerived}\,k\,(\mathcal{C}^p) = 0
3271	      \qquad \text{for all } k \geq 1.
3272	  \]
3273	\end{lemma}
3274	
3275	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "According to
3276	% Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-describe-higher-direct-images}
3277	% the sheaf
3278	% $R^if_*\mathcal{F}$ is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
3279	% $V \mapsto H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)})$.
3280	% By assumption, whenever $V$ is affine we have that $f^{-1}(V)$ is
3281	% affine, see Morphisms, Definition \ref{morphisms-definition-affine}.
3282	% By Lemma \ref{lemma-quasi-coherent-affine-cohomology-zero} we conclude that
3283	% $H^i(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{F}|_{f^{-1}(V)}) = 0$
3284	% whenever $V$ is affine. Since $S$ has a basis consisting of affine
3285	% opens we win."
3286	\begin{proof}
3287	  \uses{lem:affine_serre_vanishing, def:cech_nerve, lem:higher_direct_image_presheaf, lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp}
3288	  A product of acyclic objects is acyclic, so it suffices to treat a single factor
3289	  \((j_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s})\), where \(s = (i_0, \ldots, i_p)\), \(U_s =
3290	  U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is affine, and \(j_s : U_s \hookrightarrow X\) is the open
3291	  immersion. Acyclicity for \(f_*\) is a local question on \(S\): the derived
3292	  pushforward \(R^k f_*\, \mathcal{G}\) is the sheaf associated to the presheaf
3293	  \(V \mapsto H^k(f^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}(V)})\), so it vanishes for
3294	  \(k \geq 1\) iff that cohomology vanishes for all affine \(V \subseteq S\).
3295	  Restricting to such a \(V = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\), the base change \(f_V :
3296	  f^{-1}(V) \to V\) is again separated and quasi-compact over the separated base
3297	  \(V\), hence \(f^{-1}(V)\) is separated and the affine \(U_s\) meets it in the
3298	  affine open \(U_s \cap f^{-1}(V)\).
3299	
3300	  Now decompose the structure map \(g_s := f \circ j_s : U_s \to S\). First,
3301	  \(j_s\) is the inclusion of an affine open into the separated scheme \(X\), hence
3302	  an affine morphism, so the higher direct images \(R^q (j_s)_*\) vanish for
3303	  \(q \geq 1\). By Lemma~\ref{lem:open_immersion_pushforward_comp} part (2),
3304	  \(R^k f_*\bigl((j_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s})\bigr) \cong
3305	  R^k (g_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s})\). Second, working locally over an affine
3306	  \(V \subseteq S\) as above, \(g_s\) restricts to a morphism of affine schemes
3307	  \(U_s \cap f^{-1}(V) \to V\), which is an affine morphism; by the relative affine
3308	  vanishing \(R^k (g_s)_*(\mathcal{F}|_{U_s}) = 0\) for \(k \geq 1\). That relative
3309	  vanishing is itself a consequence of Serre vanishing
3310	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_serre_vanishing}): \(R^k(g_s)_*\mathcal{G}\) is the
3311	  sheafification of \(V \mapsto H^k(g_s^{-1}(V), \mathcal{G})\), and for affine
3312	  \(V\) the preimage \(g_s^{-1}(V)\) is affine, on which higher cohomology of a
3313	  quasi-coherent sheaf vanishes. Combining, \(R^k f_*(\mathcal{C}^p) = 0\) for all
3314	  \(k \geq 1\).
3315	\end{proof}

3317	\begin{lemma}
3318	
3319	\leanok
3320	[{\v C}ech complex computes the higher direct images]
3321	  \label{lem:cech_computes_cohomology}
3322	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.cech_computes_higherDirectImage}
3323	  \uses{lem:cech_augmented_resolution, lem:cech_term_pushforward_acyclic, lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived, def:cech_complex, def:higher_direct_image}
3324	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Tag 02KE; Lemma
3325	  % \texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent} ({\v C}ech computes cohomology when
3326	  % all intersections are affine) and Lemma
3327	  % \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application}
3328	  % ($H^q(X,\mathcal{F}) = H^0(S, R^q f_*\mathcal{F})$ for affine $S$)
3329	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L245--256 and L843--854).
3330	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $X$ be a scheme.
3331	  % Let $\mathcal{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i$ be an open covering such that
3332	  % $U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}$ is affine open for all $p \ge 0$ and all
3333	  % $i_0, \ldots, i_p \in I$.
3334	  % In this case for any quasi-coherent sheaf $\mathcal{F}$ we have
3335	  % $$
3336	  % \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) = H^p(X, \mathcal{F})
3337	  % $$
3338	  % as $\Gamma(X, \mathcal{O}_X)$-modules for all $p$."
3339	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.
3340	  % Assume that $f$ is quasi-separated and quasi-compact.
3341	  % Assume $S$ is affine.
3342	  % For any quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module $\mathcal{F}$
3343	  % we have
3344	  % $$
3345	  % H^q(X, \mathcal{F}) = H^0(S, R^qf_*\mathcal{F})
3346	  % $$
3347	  % for all $q \in \mathbf{Z}$."
3348	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Tag 02KE
3349	  (\texttt{lemma-cech-cohomology-quasi-coherent}) and
3350	  \texttt{lemma-quasi-coherence-higher-direct-images-application}.}
3351	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be a separated quasi-compact morphism of schemes, let
3352	  \(\mathcal{F}\) be a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and let
3353	  \(\mathfrak{U} : X = \bigcup_{i \in I} U_i\) be a finite affine open cover of
3354	  \(X\) (so, by separatedness, every intersection \(U_{i_0 \ldots i_p}\) is
3355	  affine). Then the cohomology sheaves of the relative {\v C}ech complex
3356	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\) of
3357	  Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex} compute the higher direct images: for every
3358	  \(i \geq 0\) there is a canonical isomorphism of \(\mathcal{O}_S\)-modules
3359	  \[
3360	    H^i\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\bigr)
3361	      \;\cong\; R^i f_*\mathcal{F}.
3362	  \]
3363	  In particular, over an affine base \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\), taking global
3364	  sections gives \(H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) = \check{H}^i(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F}) =
3365	  H^0(S, R^i f_*\mathcal{F})\) as \(A\)-modules for all \(i\).
3366	
3367	  Two points about the precise form of the comparison should be noted. First, the
3368	  derived-functor higher direct image \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) on the right-hand side
3369	  is the one obtained from injective resolutions, which is available only when the
3370	  category of \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-modules has enough injectives; accordingly this
3371	  comparison is asserted under the hypothesis that injective resolutions exist in
3372	  \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\), and it compares the (always-defined) {\v C}ech higher
3373	  direct image \(H^i(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F}))\)
3374	  against the derived-functor one only in that case. Second, we assert the comparison in the
3375	  weak form of the \emph{existence} of an isomorphism, i.e.\ as
3376	  \(\bigl(H^i(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})) \cong
3377	  R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\bigr)\) without singling out a chosen natural isomorphism.
3378	  This existence statement is all the downstream consumers (representability of the
3379	  relative Picard functor, local triviality of the comparison) require: they use
3380	  only that the unconditional {\v C}ech \(R^i f_*\) and the derived-functor
3381	  \(R^i f_*\) agree as objects, never a specific identification, so committing to a
3382	  canonical natural transformation would add coherence obligations that play no
3383	  role in the applications.
3384	\end{lemma}

3386	\begin{proof}
3387	  \uses{lem:cech_augmented_resolution, lem:cech_term_pushforward_acyclic, lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived, def:cech_complex, def:higher_direct_image}
3388	  Apply the abstract Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived} with the
3389	  right-derivable additive functor \(G = f_*\)
3390	  (the pushforward \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X) \to \operatorname{QCoh}(S)\)), the object
3391	  \(A = \mathcal{F}\), and the resolution \(J^\bullet = \mathcal{C}^\bullet\) given by
3392	  the {\v C}ech nerve of Definition~\ref{def:cech_nerve}. The two hypotheses of the
3393	  abstract lemma are exactly the geometric sub-lemmas established above:
3394	  \begin{enumerate}
3395	    \item By Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_augmented_resolution} the augmented {\v C}ech
3396	      complex \(0 \to \mathcal{F} \to \mathcal{C}^0 \to \mathcal{C}^1 \to \cdots\) is
3397	      exact, i.e.\ \(\mathcal{C}^\bullet\) is a resolution of \(\mathcal{F}\) in
3398	      \(\operatorname{QCoh}(X)\).
3399	    \item By Lemma~\ref{lem:cech_term_pushforward_acyclic} each term
3400	      \(\mathcal{C}^p\) is right \(f_*\)-acyclic:
3401	      \((f_*).\mathrm{rightDerived}\,k\,(\mathcal{C}^p) = 0\) for \(k \geq 1\).
3402	  \end{enumerate}
3403	  Lemma~\ref{lem:acyclic_resolution_computes_derived} then gives, for every \(i\), an
3404	  isomorphism between the \(i\)-th derived functor evaluated at \(\mathcal{F}\) and
3405	  the \(i\)-th cohomology of the complex obtained by applying \(f_*\) to the
3406	  resolution,
3407	  \[
3408	    (f_*).\mathrm{rightDerived}\,i\,(\mathcal{F})
3409	      \;\cong\;
3410	    H^i\bigl(f_*\,\mathcal{C}^\bullet\bigr).
3411	  \]
3412	  By construction (Definition~\ref{def:cech_complex}) the complex \(f_*\,
3413	  \mathcal{C}^\bullet\) is precisely the relative {\v C}ech complex
3414	  \(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\), so its \(i\)-th
3415	  cohomology is \(H^i\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})
3416	  \bigr)\); and \((f_*).\mathrm{rightDerived}\,i\,(\mathcal{F})\) is the
3417	  derived-functor higher direct image \(R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) of
3418	  Definition~\ref{def:higher_direct_image}. This yields the isomorphism
3419	  \(H^i\bigl(\check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F})\bigr) \cong
3420	  R^i f_*\mathcal{F}\) asserted by the lemma; taking it in the existence
3421	  (\(\operatorname{Nonempty}\)) form discards the choice of resolution, as required.
3422	  Over an affine base \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\), passing to global sections
3423	  recovers \(H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) = \check{H}^i(\mathfrak{U}, \mathcal{F}) = H^0(S,
3424	  R^i f_*\mathcal{F})\).
3425	\end{proof}
3426

# Blueprint Review — iter-023

**Slug:** blueprint-reviewer-iter023  
**Iteration:** 023  
**Reviewer role:** whole-blueprint audit + HARD GATE for two prover files  
**Chapters audited:** 3 (all)

---

## Per-chapter checklist

### 1. `Cohomology_AcyclicResolution.tex` (P4)

|---|---|
| All lemmas have `\lean{}` pins | Yes |
| All cited labels resolve (`\uses{}`) | Yes — 0 unknown_uses from leandag |
| `\leanok` / `\mathlibok` markers consistent | Yes — all blocks marked |
| Proof sketches detailed enough to formalize | Yes — P4 is done (axiom-clean) |
| Citation discipline (`SOURCE:` / `SOURCE QUOTE:`) | Yes — present on sourced blocks |
| No isolated nodes | Yes — 0 isolated |

No must-fix-this-iter findings.

---

### 2. `Cohomology_HigherDirectImage.tex` (P5a leaf, 01XJ engine)

|---|---|

| All cited labels resolve (`\uses{}`) | Yes — 0 unknown_uses |
| `\leanok` present on proved decls | Yes |
| Proof sketches sufficient | Yes — P5a leaf axiom-clean |
| No isolated nodes | Yes |

**`complete: true` | `correct: true`**

No must-fix-this-iter findings.

---

### 3. `Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex` (consolidated, `% archon:covers` 6 files)

Covers: `CechHigherDirectImage.lean`, `CechAcyclic.lean`, `PresheafCech.lean`, `FreePresheafComplex.lean`, `CechBridge.lean`, `HigherDirectImagePresheaf.lean`.

| Check | Status |
|---|---|
| `% archon:covers` declared + covers correct files | Yes |
| All `\uses{}` edges resolve | Yes — 0 unknown_uses |
| No isolated blueprint nodes | Yes — 0 isolated |
| No orphan chapters | Yes — blueprint-doctor: `"orphan_chapters": []` |
| No broken `\ref{}` / `\uses{}` | Yes — blueprint-doctor: `"broken_refs": []` |
| No malformed refs | Yes — `"malformed_refs": []` |
| No covers-mapping problems | Yes — `"covers_problems": []` |
| Citation discipline | Yes — `SOURCE:` / `SOURCE QUOTE:` / `SOURCE QUOTE PROOF:` present on every sourced block in the HARD GATE blocks |
| Proof sketches sufficient for formalization | See HARD GATE detail below |

**`complete: true` | `correct: true`**

---

## HARD GATE — FreePresheafComplex.lean

**Verdict: CLEARED**

### `lem:cech_engine_complex`

- **14 engine declarations** all listed: `cechEngineObject`, `cechEngineMap`, `cechEngineComplex`, `cechEngineDiff_sq`, `cechEngineContracting`, `cechEngineExact`, `cechEngineZero`, `cechEngineAugmentation`, `cechFreeSimplicial`, `cechFreeSimplicialAugmented`, `cechFreePresheafComplex`, `cechFreeComplex_augmented`, `cechFreeComplex_exact`, `cechFreeComplex_zero`.
- `\lean{}` pin: `cechEngineComplex` (existing decl, axiom-clean in FreePresheafComplex.lean).
- `\uses{lem:free_cech_engine}` — label exists, resolves.
- Proof sketch: complete. Variance discussion (chain complex lowers face index; coproduct dual) fully documented.
- **No non-existent `\lean{}` pin.**

### `lem:cech_free_eval_engine_iso`

- `\lean{}` pin: `cechFreeEvalEngineIso` — the declared prover target for this iter; correctly identified as the remaining blocker.
- `\uses{lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise, lem:free_cech_engine, lem:cech_engine_complex}` — all 3 labels exist and resolve.
- 3-layer proof sketch: (L1) sectionwise iso `cechFreeEval_X ≅ cechEngineComplex_X`; (L2) naturality in `X` (coherence square via Fin.cons decomposition); (L3) transport to chain-complex iso via uniqueness of the differential. The variance bottleneck is explicitly addressed (DFin vs Fin, biproduct vs product, coface vs face duality).
- **Complete and correct.**

### `lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy`

- `% NOTE:` annotation explicitly documents: **no standalone `\lean{}` pin by design** — this block is a transport corollary of the engine iso and the homotopy already proved; it does not correspond to a new Lean declaration.
- `\uses{lem:cech_free_eval_engine_iso, lem:cech_engine_complex, lem:free_cech_engine, lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise}` — all 4 labels exist and resolve.
- Proof sketch describes the `prepend` algebra: transport the contracting homotopy through the engine→eval iso.
- **Complete and correct. Intentional pin absence is documented.**

### `lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy_spec`

- Same design decision: **no standalone `\lean{}` pin** (% NOTE present), same rationale.
- `\uses{lem:cech_free_eval_engine_iso, lem:cech_engine_complex, lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy, lem:free_cech_engine}` — all 4 labels resolve.
- **Complete and correct. Intentional pin absence is documented.**

### `lem:cech_free_eval_nonempty`

- `\lean{}` pin: `cechFreeEval_quasiIso_of_nonempty` — the declared prover target (nonempty glue step).
- `\uses{lem:cech_free_eval_engine_iso, lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy, lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy_spec, lem:cech_engine_complex, lem:free_cech_engine}` — all 5 labels resolve.
- Proof sketch: transport quasi-iso from the augmented engine complex to the eval complex when the cover is nonempty. Covers the `Fin.cons`/`h0` branch cleanly.
- **Complete and correct.**

### `lem:cech_free_complex_quasi_iso`

- `\lean{}` pin: `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso` — the final P3b free-side target.
- `\uses{lem:cech_free_eval_nonempty, lem:cech_free_eval_engine_iso, lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy, lem:cech_free_eval_prepend_homotopy_spec, lem:cech_engine_complex, lem:free_cech_engine, lem:cech_free_eval_sectionwise}` — all 7 labels resolve.
- Full `SOURCE QUOTE PROOF` present (Stacks reference).
- Proof sketch: reduce to `cechFreeEval_quasiIso_of_nonempty` (cover non-empty by assumption) + assemble via nonempty augmentation.
- **Complete and correct.**

**Prior iter "6 blueprint-side majors" confirmed RESOLVED**: leandag reports 0 `unknown_uses`. Every `\uses{}` in the 6 HARD GATE blocks now resolves.

---

## HARD GATE — CechBridge.lean

**Verdict: CLEARED**

### `lem:ses_cech_h1`

- `\lean{}` pin: `ses_cech_h1`.
- `\uses{def:cech_complex}` — **only one** dependency, resolves. Does NOT use `injective_cech_acyclic`, `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`, or any other active prover target — self-contained as required.
- Statement block: verbatim source quote from `stacks-cohomology.tex` (`lemma-ses-cech-h1`), correctly attributed.
- Proof block: `SOURCE QUOTE PROOF` present, full Stacks argument (surjectivity on sections from Ȟ¹(𝒰,F)=0 via exactness of the degree-0/1 segment), sufficiently detailed for direct Lean formalization.
- Hypothesis: Ȟ¹(𝒰,F)=0 taken as a `h : IsZero ((cechComplex 𝒰 F).homology 1)` parameter — no circularity with `injective_cech_acyclic` or `cechFreeComplex_quasiIso`.
- **Complete, correct, and self-contained.**

---

## leandag / blueprint-doctor summary

```
unknown_uses : 0          ← no broken \uses{} edges
isolated     : 0          ← no orphan blueprint nodes
broken_refs  : []         ← blueprint-doctor clean
covers_problems: []       ← archon:covers mapping correct
```

`unmatched_lean` list (22 entries) is expected and non-blocking: entries are either unproven project targets (the prover's job this or future iters) or `\mathlibok` Mathlib items not expected in project Lean files.

---

## Must-fix-this-iter findings

**None.**

---

## Unstarted-phase blueprint proposals

**None.** All strategy phases have blueprint coverage:

- **P3** (`CechAcyclic.lean`) — active, chapter present, HARD GATE previously cleared.
- **P3b** (`PresheafCech.lean`, `FreePresheafComplex.lean`, `CechBridge.lean`) — active, chapter present, HARD GATE CLEARED this iter.
- **P5a** (`HigherDirectImagePresheaf.lean`) — active, chapter present, HARD GATE previously cleared (P5a leaf axiom-clean).
- **P5b** — blocked, but `cech_computes_higherDirectImage` assembly block present in chapter.

---

## Severity summary

| Severity | Count |
|---|---|
| Must-fix-this-iter (blocking HARD GATE) | 0 |
| Should-fix (non-blocking, next writer pass) | 0 |
| Informational | 0 |

---

## Overall verdict

**HARD GATE: CLEARED for both `FreePresheafComplex.lean` and `CechBridge.lean`.**

Both files may proceed to prover dispatch this iter. All `\uses{}` edges resolve. All `\lean{}` pins are either existing axiom-clean declarations (engine complex) or correctly identified as the prover's targets for this iter. The two intentionally pin-free homotopy-spec blocks are correctly annotated with `% NOTE:`. `ses_cech_h1` is self-contained and carries no circular dependency.
